title: Platform foundation
description: Vite + React + TypeScript app shell with PWA, native wrappers, storage, and privacy defaults. Everything every other epic stands on.

title: Vite + React + TS scaffold
description: Initialize the project with Vite, React 18+, TypeScript strict, and a clean monorepo-friendly layout. ESLint + Prettier + tsconfig with path aliases. Commit a hello-world that boots in <2s on cold reload.
design: Use Vite 5+. Workspace structure: app/ (UI), engine/ (rendering + runtime), content/ (scenarios), tools/ (CLI authoring). Path aliases via tsconfig + vite-tsconfig-paths.
acceptance: `npm run dev` boots in <2s. `npm run build` produces a working static bundle. TS strict mode passes with zero errors.

title: PWA + service worker
description: Add vite-plugin-pwa with Workbox runtime caching, a complete manifest.json (icons, theme, kid-friendly screenshots), install-to-homescreen affordance. Cache-first for static, network-first for API stubs.
design: Workbox via vite-plugin-pwa. Don't write the SW by hand. Test offline mode by toggling Chrome DevTools network to offline after first load — full app should still work.
acceptance: App installs to homescreen on iOS Safari and Android Chrome. After first visit + going offline, all currently-installed scenarios still load and play.

title: Capacitor wrappers (iOS, Android)
description: Wrap the PWA in Capacitor for App Store + Play distribution. Native bridges for camera, geolocation, file system. Kids Category / Designed for Families config templates.
design: Capacitor 6+. Use Capacitor's official Camera and Geolocation plugins; do not roll your own. Defer push notifications.
acceptance: `npx cap run ios` produces a runnable iOS build. `npx cap run android` produces an Android APK. Camera and GPS permissions work via native dialog.

title: Storage layer (Dexie + OPFS + SQLite-WASM)
description: Three-store storage layer behind a single TypeScript interface: Dexie for app state, OPFS for blobs >=10MB, sqlocal/wa-sqlite for queryable species data. Includes a quota observer and graceful degradation.
design: Single Storage facade with three backing stores. `navigator.storage.persist()` requested at first scenario load, not at app launch. Quota observer warns the user well before eviction risk.
acceptance: Round-trip a 50MB binary blob through OPFS in <500ms on a mid-range tablet. Query a 10K-row species DB in <50ms. Quota observer fires a callback at 80% used.

title: Privacy defaults + permission scaffolding
description: Default-private architecture: photos local-only, voice off, location obscured to 100m, no telemetry, no analytics SDKs. Just-in-time permission prompts wrapped in a kid-friendly explainer overlay.
design: Permission UX: an in-app explainer (illustration + 1-sentence reason + parent-narrator voice-over) runs BEFORE the OS-level prompt. Never ask for a permission a scenario step doesn't need at that moment.
acceptance: No permissions requested at app launch. Camera prompt only fires from a field-activity step. GPS only from a step that bounds distance. Coordinates never persisted at higher than 100m precision.

title: Engine and rendering
description: Three.js + R3F for 3D, PixiJS for 2D overlays, Rapier for the small amount of physics needed in MVP, asset loading pipeline.

title: Three.js + R3F integration
description: Set up react-three-fiber with @react-three/drei utilities and @react-three/xr (XR deferred but configured). Configure WebGL2 with KTX2 + Draco loaders. One walkable test scene.
design: R3F in a Suspense boundary with a child-friendly loading state. KTX2Loader and DRACOLoader configured at app level; scenes import via useGLTF.
acceptance: Test scene: a glTF cube on a glTF plane, rotation animated, KTX2 texture, Draco-compressed mesh. Renders at 60fps on iPad 9th gen in landscape.

title: PixiJS 2D overlay
description: PixiJS layered above the 3D canvas for HUD, narrative overlays, tap-to-learn fact cards. Coordinate input routing between 2D and 3D layers.
design: PixiJS in a transparent canvas overlay. Pointer events: 2D first, fallthrough to 3D if no 2D hit. Coordinate space: PixiJS uses screen pixels; pass through R3F's useThree() projection for any 2D-to-3D mapping.
acceptance: A fact card overlay can be opened from a 3D object tap, dismissed by tapping outside, and renders crisply on a high-DPI tablet.

title: Asset loading pipeline (glTF + KTX2 + Draco)
description: Asset bundle loader with progress events, lazy-loaded per scenario, OPFS-backed for cached scenarios. Audio also pipelined (Opus). Integrity checks via hashed manifests.
design: A scenario's bundle manifest lists files + sizes + hashes. Loader: hit OPFS first, network second, write to OPFS on miss. Web worker for hash verification.
acceptance: A 30MB scenario bundle loads from OPFS in <1.5s on a mid-range Android tablet. Cache hit ratio >95% after first install.

title: Audio playback layer
description: Web Audio API wrapper for VO, SFX, ambient. Per-track volume controls, ducking, voice-over priority bus. Honors silent-mode and headphone routing.
design: Three buses: voice (priority, ducks ambient), SFX (UI feedback + scene), ambient (loops). Use Howler.js as the wrapper unless a hand-rolled layer is justified.
acceptance: Ambient duck triggers within 50ms of VO start, restores within 200ms of VO end. iOS silent mode respected (audio still routes correctly to headphones).

title: Rapier physics (light)
description: Rapier 3D via @react-three/rapier for the very small amount of physics needed in MVP scenarios (e.g., a beetle hotel item dropping into place). SIMD variant on capable browsers.
design: Most MVP scenarios are observation-driven, not physics-driven. Configure Rapier minimally; expand only when a scenario explicitly needs it.
acceptance: Rapier loaded; a single demo scene with rigid bodies works at 60fps. Scenarios without physics do NOT load Rapier (lazy import).

title: Scenario runtime
description: The state machine and content layer that turns a scenario manifest plus assets into a playable loop. Manifest schema, loop XState, skill-rung resolver, mentor-apprentice seat config, Ink narrative integration.

title: Manifest schema + parser
description: Implement the scenario manifest schema from biotope-mvp-planning.md section 2 as a TypeScript-typed YAML/JSON loader with a JSON Schema for validation. Round-trip stable.
design: Use `zod` for runtime validation; codegen TS types from the same source. YAML via `yaml` package. Manifest version 1 freezes per the planning doc.
acceptance: The full Backyard Bird Hour manifest example from the planning doc parses without errors and round-trips byte-stable through the loader.

title: Loop state machine (XState)
description: XState statechart for the five-phase loop: brief → sim → field → re-encoding → reflection. Pauseable, resumable, persisted to storage. Hooks for narrative-engine driving each transition.
design: XState v5. The same statechart is used client-side and (later) server-side for multiplayer. State persistence to Dexie on every transition.
acceptance: A scenario can be paused mid-sim, app force-killed, reopened, and resumes at the exact same step with all state intact.

title: Skill rung resolver
description: Resolves which content variant (5-6, 7-8, 9-10, 11-12) applies based on the active player profile, then patches the manifest with skill_rung_overrides. Reading-level-aware UI mode switching.
design: Pure function: (manifest, profile) => resolved-manifest. UI mode (pre_reader / early_reader / fluent_simple / fluent) is part of the resolved output and drives a top-level CSS class.
acceptance: Same manifest renders three demonstrably different experiences for ages 6, 9, and 11 with no manifest edit.

title: Mentor + apprentice seat config
description: Two-seat UI: parent overlay during sim and field, coaching prompts on the parent's track only, photo-gate handoff. Solo mode is a degenerate two-seat with the parent seat hidden.
design: One device or two devices on the same Wi-Fi (LAN peer for v1.1; v1 single-device co-play). Parent overlay is a slide-out panel toggled by long-press of a corner badge.
acceptance: Backyard Bird Hour played in mentor+apprentice mode shows parent coaching at the right beats, and the photo-gate prompt is parent-confirmable.

title: Ink narrative engine integration
description: Bind inkjs into the runtime. Each manifest references Ink nodes; transitions between loop phases call into Ink for the spoken/displayed content.
design: inkjs latest. Compile .ink to .json at scenario build time; runtime loads the .json. Pass external functions (e.g., `getPlayerName`, `getRecentObservation`) into the Ink story.
acceptance: A two-branch Ink scene runs end-to-end with VO triggered, choices made, and external functions called.

title: Scenario lifecycle (load / save / unlock)
description: The chain that loads a scenario from a content pack, saves player progress, and unlocks the next scenario. Free vs subscriber gating is part of this layer.
design: Scenario state in Dexie keyed by (scenarioId, playerProfileId). Unlock chain declared in manifest's reflection.unlocks.next_scenario. Tier gating (free/digital/hybrid) checked at load.
acceptance: Window Watch completion auto-unlocks Backyard Bird Hour. Hybrid-tier scenarios are gated for non-hybrid subscribers with a clear (non-pushy) upgrade path.

title: Field activity layer
description: Camera capture with parent gate, GPS distance bounding, indoor fallback handoff, and re-encoding of field outputs back into the sim.

title: Camera + photo capture
description: Native camera via Capacitor + a PWA fallback via getUserMedia. Photos saved to OPFS by default, never uploaded without explicit consent.
design: Capacitor Camera plugin in native; getUserMedia + canvas in PWA. EXIF stripped on capture (location, device fingerprint). Strip to JPEG max 1600px long edge to keep storage tight.
acceptance: Photo captured, stripped of EXIF, stored to OPFS. Round-trip reads in <100ms. PWA fallback works on a Pixel running Chrome.

title: GPS bounding for safety
description: Scenario-declared max_distance_from_origin_m enforced by Geolocation watchPosition. Soft warn parent at 80% of bound; pause activity at bound. GPS coords obscured to 100m before any storage.
design: watchPosition with adaptive accuracy (lower power when stationary). Warning UX is parent-track only; the child sees a friendly 'time to head back' nudge from the narrator.
acceptance: With a 50m bound, a tester walking 60m from origin gets a parent warning and a child-track narrator nudge within 5 seconds.

title: Photo gating for parent
description: Photos captured during mentor+apprentice mode are held in a pending state until the parent confirms; the child sees a 'showing this to your grown-up' state. Below age 9 the gate is forced on; 9-12 the gate is configurable.
design: Pending state visible to both seats; parent confirms with a single tap on their seat overlay. Pending photos are still saved to OPFS but flagged not-yet-confirmed.
acceptance: Child captures a photo; parent overlay shows confirm/discard; on confirm, photo flows to re-encoding; on discard, photo is purged from OPFS.

title: Indoor fallback handling
description: When weather, time, or attention forces an indoor reroute, the manifest's fallback_indoor narrative node runs and the loop continues without scolding. Failure-mode UX is generous.
design: Triggers: parent override, weather check, 30-min timeout, 3+ failed attempts in a row. Re-encoding accepts a self-reported observation in lieu of a photo.
acceptance: A scenario completed entirely indoors via fallback still produces a re-encoded result and unlocks the next scenario.

title: Re-encoding into sim
description: The manifest's re_encoding.accept array drives what is accepted from the field (photo with subject hint, self-reported ID). The sim_response action runs in the 3D scene and the narrative engine ties it off.
design: For MVP the local recognition model can be very light or even absent — accept self-reported IDs and a low-confidence photo classifier as parallel paths. Both feed the same sim_response.
acceptance: A photo of a bird (any) results in 'place_player_observation_in_scene' running and the bird-mascot returning to the 3D backyard scene.

title: Locality and maps
description: MapLibre GL + PMTiles for offline regional maps; locality-aware scene parameters (season, daypart); weather lookup with offline cache.

title: MapLibre GL JS + PMTiles integration
description: MapLibre rendering a PMTiles archive served from OPFS. The 'where am I' map shown briefly during onboarding and used by scenarios that opt in.
design: PMTiles via the official @protomaps/basemaps + pmtiles JS package. Style: light theme, kid-friendly. The map is a contextual element; not the primary surface.
acceptance: A 50MB PMTiles archive of LA County loads from OPFS and renders smoothly at zoom 8-14 on a mid-range Android tablet.

title: First regional PMTiles extract
description: Build a tippecanoe + go-pmtiles pipeline that produces a Greater LA Area regional pack (target <100MB) from current OSM extracts.
design: Use Geofabrik's California extract, clip to the Greater LA bbox, run tippecanoe with a basemap-friendly schema, convert to PMTiles. Build script is part of the repo.
acceptance: `npm run build:region la-greater` produces a versioned .pmtiles file <100MB and metadata JSON.

title: Locality-aware scene parameters
description: At scenario load, look up player locality (climate zone, season, sunrise/sunset, current daypart) and pass them as scene parameters. Different lighting / fauna / phenology per locality and time.
design: Locality service: input lat/lon, output { climate_zone, season, daypart, sunrise, sunset, koppen_class }. Internal lookup table for climate zones; SunCalc for sun timings.
acceptance: Backyard Bird Hour played at 9am in Pasadena vs 5pm in Boston shows different scene lighting, different default bird species lists, different narrator framings.

title: Weather lookup integration
description: Weather check before suggesting a field activity. Online via Open-Meteo (free, no key); offline-cached snapshot used when network is absent.
design: Open-Meteo API. Cache the last 48h forecast on each successful fetch. Map weather codes to manifest weather_unsuitable categories.
acceptance: With network on, current weather is fetched in <1s. With network off, the most recent cached forecast is used; if too stale (>24h), default to 'safe to go outside' with a parent advisory.

title: Species data layer
description: Region-specific species packs in SQLite-WASM, online iNaturalist fallback, light on-device species recognition for re-encoding.

title: Region pack format (SQLite-WASM)
description: Define and implement the .biotope-region pack format: a SQLite database (taxa, common_names, photos_manifest, audio_manifest, occurrences_summary) plus a sidecar of binary assets. Schema versioned.
design: sqlocal for the SQLite-WASM runtime. Pack format = a directory containing schema.sql, data.sqlite, and assets/. Schema documented in /docs/region-pack-format.md (to be authored as part of this task).
acceptance: A region pack with 100 species + 3 photos each + 30 calls round-trips through the loader and answers `find taxa near (lat,lon)` in <50ms.

title: First region species pack (LA-area)
description: Hand-curate a Greater LA region pack: ~150 common species across birds, mammals, reptiles, common plants, and common insects. Sources: iNaturalist research-grade observations + GBIF + Audubon LA. License-clean photos.
design: Build script `tools/region-pack` ingests an iNat export + GBIF DwC-A + a curation CSV; outputs the .biotope-region pack. Photos are CC-BY-licensed iNat research-grade only.
acceptance: `la-greater-v1.biotope-region` exists, is <50MB, includes the 25 species needed by the five MVP scenarios, and passes all package validators.

title: Online iNaturalist fallback
description: When online and a player taps a species not in their region pack, query the iNaturalist API and surface the result with a 'not in your area' indicator. Rate-limited per the iNat docs.
design: iNat API at api.inaturalist.org/v1/. Rate cap: 60 req/min per app. Cache results to Dexie; never make a request the user didn't initiate.
acceptance: With network on, a query for an out-of-region species returns within 2s and is cached. With network off, the user gets a friendly 'we'll learn this one when we're online' message.

title: Local on-device species recognition (light)
description: A lightweight image classifier that returns 'is this a bird? a plant? a mammal? a reptile? unknown?' with a confidence — NOT full species ID. Used for re-encoding low-bar acceptance.
design: Tiny TF.js or ONNX model, target <5MB. Fine-tuned on iNat coarse labels (kingdom/class). Run in a worker, not the main thread.
acceptance: Coarse classification of a typical phone photo runs in <500ms on a 2022 mid-range Android tablet with >85% accuracy on bird vs plant vs mammal vs reptile.

title: Authoring tooling
description: create-biotope-scenario CLI scaffolder, biotope-validate manifest validator, asset compression pipeline, per-rung playthrough harness.

title: create-biotope-scenario CLI scaffolder
description: `npx create-biotope-scenario <slug>` produces the scenario folder layout from biotope-mvp-planning.md with stub manifest, narrative, asset folders, and a README.
design: Tiny Node CLI; templates in /tools/scenario-template/. Interactive prompts for title, age rungs, modes, biome.
acceptance: `npx create-biotope-scenario test-scenario` produces a scaffold that immediately passes biotope-validate.

title: biotope-validate manifest validator
description: `biotope-validate <path>` runs schema validation, asset budget checks, missing-narrative-node checks, missing-localization checks, and a per-rung dry-run.
design: Reuse the zod schema from bd-rntm.1. Asset checks via stat. Narrative checks by parsing Ink and verifying every referenced node exists.
acceptance: Catches a manifest that references a missing Ink node with a clear, actionable error message; catches an over-budget asset bundle.

title: Asset compression pipeline
description: Build script that takes raw glTF, audio, images and produces compressed shippable assets: Draco mesh + KTX2 textures, Opus audio, AVIF/WebP images.
design: Use gltfpack and gltf-transform for glTF; basisu for KTX2; ffmpeg for Opus; sharp for AVIF/WebP. Wrapped in a single `npm run build:scenario <slug>` command.
acceptance: A 50MB raw scenario bundle compresses to <15MB shippable, passes visual diff at acceptable thresholds, plays back identically.

title: Per-rung playthrough harness
description: Headless harness that boots a scenario for each declared (rung, mode) combination and asserts the loop completes end-to-end. Catches age-rung-specific regressions.
design: Playwright + a deterministic test harness. Mock camera, GPS, and network. Capture screenshots per rung for visual review.
acceptance: `npm run test:scenarios` runs all five MVP scenarios across all declared (rung, mode) combinations and produces a report.

title: MVP scenarios (5)
description: Author the five MVP scenarios from biotope-mvp-planning.md section 1: Window Watch, Backyard Bird Hour, Whose Tracks?, Leaf Detective, Pond Window. Linear unlock chain.

title: Window Watch (intro scenario)
description: ~10 min total. The universal first scenario; works from any window. No outdoor required, no kit required, no GPS required. Designed to onboard the loop in the smallest possible footprint.
design: Sim: a stylized window with three bird-cards rotating in. Field: 'spend 2 minutes looking out a real window — anything count.' Re-encoding: self-report 'did you see anything?' with thumbs. Reflection: a single 'see you tomorrow' beat.
acceptance: Plays end-to-end across all four age rungs in mentor+apprentice mode. Works in solo for 9-10 and 11-12. Asset bundle <15MB.

title: Backyard Bird Hour
description: ~25 min for 11-12, 15 min for 5-6. Three local birds in a 3D backyard scene, then a real outdoor 10-minute observation, then re-encoding into the scene. The flagship MVP scenario walked through in detail in the planning doc.
design: Per the manifest example in biotope-mvp-planning.md section 2-3. Three rigged 3D birds (contracted out per GTM doc), final VO contracted, original score for emotional anchor cues.
acceptance: Plays end-to-end across all four age rungs in mentor+apprentice mode. Solo mode works for 9-12. Asset bundle <30MB. Field activity has a gracefully working indoor fallback.

title: Whose Tracks?
description: Mammal tracks observation. Sim: a 3D ground scene with simulated tracks; tap to ID. Field: find any animal sign outdoors (track, scat, fur, scratch).
design: Reuse Bird Hour's authoring patterns. Tracks are 2D decals on a 3D ground plane. Self-reported ID is the primary re-encoding path; photo optional.
acceptance: Plays end-to-end across all rungs. Asset bundle <25MB.

title: Leaf Detective
description: Plant identification through leaves. Sim: identify three trees from their leaves; see how leaves vary across a tree. Field: find one leaf, photograph it.
design: Three local trees. The 3D scene is a stylized small park with three 'walkable' trees. Leaf textures on alpha-cards.
acceptance: Plays end-to-end across all rungs. Asset bundle <25MB. Local recognition (bd-spec.4) used for re-encoding when available.

title: Pond Window
description: Pond / micro-aquatic. Sim: a 'window into a pond' showing tadpoles, dragonfly nymphs, water striders. Field: any nearby water (pond, stream, fountain, puddle); 5 min of looking.
design: The most ambitious of the MVP five. May be deferred to v1.1 if MVP timeline tightens — Window Watch + Backyard Bird Hour + 2 of the others is still a respectable v1.
acceptance: Plays end-to-end across all rungs. Asset bundle <30MB. Indoor fallback uses a 'puddle / sink water' proxy.

title: Distribution
description: App Store + Play Store certification, PWA hosting, store listing creative, rollout to Canada (soft launch geography per GTM MVP doc).

title: iOS App Store kid-cert prep + submission
description: Apple Kids Category compliance, parental gate UX, App Privacy answers, screenshots + hero creative, App Store Connect submission for Canadian region.
design: Lean on the GTM doc's contractor budget for hero creative. Plan two cycles of review rejection; submit conservatively.
acceptance: App approved for sale in Canada under Kids Category 6-8 and 9-11.

title: Google Play Designed for Families prep + submission
description: Designed for Families program enrollment, Data safety form, Families Policy compliance, store listing for Canadian region.
design: Files Policy + Data safety are the main work. Avoid any third-party SDK that touches advertising.
acceptance: App live on Play in Canada under Designed for Families.

title: PWA hosting
description: Static hosting for the PWA at biotope.app (or chosen domain). CDN-fronted. Service worker scope and manifest correct.
design: Cloudflare Pages or Netlify; either is fine. Custom domain via Lights Out Games registrar.
acceptance: App accessible at biotope.app. Lighthouse PWA audit score >90. Installs to homescreen on iOS Safari and Android Chrome.

title: Privacy and legal
description: Privacy policy, terms of service, COPPA + GDPR-K compliance review, permission UX flows. Lean engagement with LA tech-startup counsel per GTM MVP.

title: Privacy policy + ToS
description: Draft, legal review, publish. Privacy policy specifically explains the local-only-by-default architecture and what (minimal) data ever leaves the device.
design: Start from a kid-app-aware template (e.g., Termly or iubenda Kids). Hand-edit. Lawyer review (~$1-2K).
acceptance: Approved by counsel. Linked from app, store listings, and website. Translated to French (Canada) before B-1 launch geography.

title: COPPA + GDPR-K compliance review
description: End-to-end audit by counsel: data flows, consent flows, deletion flows. Ensure no third-party SDK violates kid-app rules. Document findings and remediations.
design: Counsel engagement, ~$3-5K flat-rate per GTM doc. Output: a written attestation we can show Apple/Google reviewers.
acceptance: Counsel attestation in hand; all P0/P1 findings fixed; remaining findings tracked as their own bd issues.

title: Permission UX flows
description: In-app permission explainer screens, parent verification UX (email-loop COPPA-style), data deletion flow, account-free first-run.
design: Account creation deferred to first save. Parent verification = email loop (no SMS) with a magic link. Deletion is one tap from settings, irreversible after a 7-day cooling-off.
acceptance: All flows playtested with at least one alpha family. No coercive dark patterns. Reviewer-friendly screenshots prepared.
